title: e2e test mutates KUBECONFIG via set_var; unsafe in multi-threaded CI
description: tests/e2e.rs:34: unsafe { std::env::set_var('KUBECONFIG', &kubeconfig) }. The SAFETY comment claims 'single-threaded tokio test; no concurrent threads read KUBECONFIG'. But cargo test runs multiple test binaries in parallel by default. If two test processes share the env (forked), or if the tokio runtime has I/O threads that call into kube, KUBECONFIG mutation is a data race. Safer: pass kubeconfig via a tempfile + KUBECONFIG env already set before spawning the test process, or use kube::Config::from_kubeconfig() with an explicit path instead of relying on env mutation. — Risk: flaky CI test that is hard to diagnose.
